a.style: font-family:'Playfair Display', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Liberation Serif', serif

text.tspan: Countries where guinea worm disease is endemic, 2023

To be certified as free of
guinea worm disease
, a country must have reported zero indigenous cases through
active surveillance for at least three consecutive years.
Note: A disease outbreak is endemic in a country if it is consistently present in the country. Countries labeled pre-certification are endemic
countries that have reported zero indigenous cases in the calendar year. Countries labeled pending surveillance have not had sufficient
testing to determine their status or the number of cases in the country.
